items.situation: two friends debating whether a new cafe is worth the price

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you tried that new cafe on Elm Street? I went yesterday and the latte cost six dollars.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Six dollars? Wow, that's pricey. Was it actually any good?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, it tasted the same as the cheaper place near the library. I think you're paying for the fancy decor.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right. I'd rather save my money and sit somewhere plain. The library cafe has free refills too.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Exactly. I won't be going back to Elm Street. Want to grab a coffee at the library spot later instead?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sounds great. Let's meet there around three.

questions.stem: How much did the woman pay for her latte?

options.C: It was free with a refill

questions.explanation: 女士明确说拿铁花了六美元（the latte cost six dollars），所以A正确。B三美元在对话中是约定见面的时间“around three”，被错误挪用为价格，属于wrong_detail。C免费续杯说的是图书馆咖啡店的福利，并非她付的价钱，属于misattributed。D学生折扣对话中从未提及，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: What will the two speakers most likely do later?

options.A: Return to the Elm Street cafe together
options.B: Complain to the cafe about the prices
options.C: Get coffee at the library cafe
options.D: Make coffee at home to save money

questions.explanation: 女士提议晚点去图书馆咖啡店，男士回答“Sounds great. Let's meet there around three.”，所以C正确。A返回Elm Street咖啡店是女士明确拒绝的（I won't be going back），属于rejected_option。B去咖啡店投诉价格对话中没有任何依据，属于over_inference。D在家自己煮咖啡也从未被提及，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: two roommates working out a fair chore schedule

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, can we figure out a cleaning schedule? The kitchen is getting kind of messy.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, good idea. How about we split it up by week?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay. Why don't you take the kitchen this week, and I'll handle the bathroom?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That works. Then we swap next week so it's fair.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll write it on the whiteboard by the fridge so we don't forget.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Great. I'll start on the dishes tonight then.

questions.stem: Which area will the man clean this week?
questions.explanation: 女士说“you take the kitchen this week”，男士同意，所以本周男士打扫厨房，B正确。A浴室是女士本周负责的区域，属于misattributed。C整个公寓与他们分工轮流的安排相反，属于wrong_detail。D卧室对话中从未提到，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.D: Write the schedule on the whiteboard

questions.explanation: 女士说“I'll write it on the whiteboard by the fridge”，所以D正确。A自己打扫厨房与分工安排矛盾，厨房本周归男士，属于reversed_situation。B找另一个室友帮忙对话中没有出现，属于unrelated_next_step。C取消计划与她积极安排时间表的态度相反，属于over_inference。

items.situation: two friends sharing cooking tips while grocery shopping

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm grabbing these tomatoes for pasta sauce, but mine always turns out kind of watery.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, I had that problem too. The trick is to let the sauce simmer way longer than you think.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? How long are we talking?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: At least forty minutes on low heat. And add a pinch of sugar to balance the acidity.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Huh, I never knew about the sugar. I'll definitely try that tonight.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You'll see a huge difference. Grab some fresh basil too while we're here.

questions.stem: What does the man say causes the woman's sauce to be watery?

options.A: She uses the wrong kind of tomatoes
options.C: She doesn't simmer it long enough
options.D: The heat is set too high

questions.explanation: 男士说窍门是让酱汁炖得比想象的久得多（simmer way longer），至少四十分钟，说明问题在于炖煮时间不够，C正确。A番茄种类不对对话中未提及，属于related_but_not_stated。B加太多水从未被说，属于related_but_not_stated。D火太大正好相反，他建议用小火（low heat），属于wrong_detail。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do tonight?

options.A: Add a pinch of sugar to her sauce

questions.explanation: 女士说从不知道加糖这一招，并说“I'll definitely try that tonight”，所以今晚她最可能加一撮糖，A正确。B去餐厅点意面与她打算自己做饭相反，属于unrelated_next_step。C改用罐装酱汁对话中没有依据，属于unrelated_next_step。D完全不用番茄与她正在买番茄做酱矛盾，属于reversed_situation/over_inference。

items.situation: two friends recommending a podcast or TV series to each other

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You have to check out this history podcast I found. Each episode breaks down one ancient mystery.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, podcasts aren't really my thing. I usually fall asleep listening to people just talk.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I get that, but this one has sound effects and interviews, so it feels more like a story.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Okay, that does sound different. Maybe I'll give the first episode a shot on my commute.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Start with the one about the lost city. It hooked me completely.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Alright, I'll queue it up for tomorrow morning.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to record their own podcast
options.B: A history class they are both taking
options.C: The man's trouble sleeping at night
options.D: A podcast the woman is recommending

questions.explanation: 整段对话围绕女士向男士推荐一档历史播客展开，所以D正确。A录制自己的播客从未被讨论，属于wrong_topic。B两人一起上的历史课对话中没有，属于wrong_topic。C男士的睡眠问题只是他随口提到“听人讲话会睡着”的一个细节，并非主旨，属于too_narrow。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do?

options.A: Recommend a different podcast to the woman
options.B: Listen to the lost-city episode first
options.C: Stop listening to podcasts for good
options.D: Take a nap during his commute

questions.explanation: 女士让他从“the lost city”那一集开始听，男士回答“I'll queue it up for tomorrow morning”，所以他最可能先听这一集，B正确。A向女士推荐别的播客对话中没有依据，属于unrelated_next_step。C从此不再听播客与他答应试听相反，属于reversed_situation/over_inference。D通勤时打盹是他原来的担忧，但他已决定去听这集，属于rejected_option。

items.situation: two students talking about which elective class to take next term

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you picked your elective for next term yet? I can't decide between photography and astronomy.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'm leaning toward astronomy. My friend took it and said the night observation labs were amazing.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that does sound cool. But doesn't astronomy meet at eight in the morning?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, it's an early class. I don't mind, though. I'm always up early anyway.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I am definitely not a morning person, so I think I'll go with photography instead.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That makes sense. Photography's a better fit for your schedule.

questions.stem: Why did the man's friend recommend astronomy?

options.A: The night observation labs were amazing
options.B: The class meets in the morning

questions.explanation: 男士说朋友上过这门课，说夜间观测实验室很棒（the night observation labs were amazing），所以A正确。B课在早上是关于上课时间的细节，并不是朋友推荐的理由，属于misattributed。C比摄影更容易对话中从未提及，属于related_but_not_stated。D教授考试少也没有出现，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: Why will the woman most likely choose photography?

options.A: She thinks it is more useful for her major
options.B: Her friends are all taking photography
options.C: She prefers not to take an early class
options.D: The astronomy class is already full

questions.explanation: 女士说自己绝对不是早起的人（not a morning person），而天文学早上八点上课，所以她选摄影是为了避开早课，C正确。A对专业更有用对话中没有依据，属于unrelated_next_step。B朋友都选摄影从未被提及，属于related_but_not_stated。D天文学已满也没有出现，属于over_inference。
